reasoning: The Lean statement encodes the informal claim exactly. `[MetricSpace X]` captures "metric space"; `[ConnectedSpace X]` captures "connected"; `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` is the standard formalization of "more than one point"; and `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` — via the subtype coercion of `univ` — is equivalent to `¬ Countable X` (Mathlib's `countable_univ_iff`), i.e., X is uncountable. Mathlib's `Countable` typeclass includes finite sets, so `¬ Countable` corresponds exactly to the mathematical notion "uncountable" (cardinality > ℵ₀). There are no extra hypotheses narrowing the scope and no missing hypotheses weakening the conclusion.
